response.justification: Bogdan brings 20 years of broad engineering experience with genuine depth in developer tooling, CI/CD infrastructure, test frameworks, and technical mentorship, satisfying the technical prerequisites well. However, the resume contains no evidence of direct people management — no mention of managing direct reports, conducting performance reviews, hiring, or owning team-level roadmaps — which is a hard minimum requirement (2 years) for this role. Staff-level titles and workshop facilitation suggest technical leadership, but fall short of the people-management bar the JD sets. He warrants a screen call to probe whether any informal or formal management experience exists that simply was not documented.
strengths: Deep, hands-on developer-tooling background: CI servers, test runners (Trial), assertion libraries (Fluent-Asserts), mobile device orchestration, and MCP code-generation frameworks
Demonstrated teaching and knowledge-sharing culture via Selenium courses, Refactoring Dojos, Code Kata sessions, and conference talks — a strong signal for team development instincts
Broad cross-platform technical range (web, mobile iOS/Apple TV, embedded, distributed systems) that aligns with the multi-surface mobile platform ecosystem described in the JD
concerns: No explicit people management experience anywhere in the resume — no direct reports, no hiring ownership, no performance-review accountability mentioned across any role
No evidence of organizational change leadership or owning a multi-quarter team roadmap at management scope (preferred: 3 years)
No advanced degree (Master's/PhD preferred) and current role framing remains individual-contributor ('full-stack engineer'), suggesting no active transition toward management
